label: GeoNamesRestriction
description: When applying a geographical names restriction, the resource must be explicitly associated with the concept scheme in which it is included. Specifically, you must provide at least one direct link to a concept scheme, which serves as a set of defined concepts, and this specific identifier URL must be exactly the GeoNames registry located at http://sws.geonames.org.

label: ContinentRestriction
description: When establishing a continent restriction for a resource, it must be explicitly included within a formal concept scheme, which acts as a controlled set of concepts. To satisfy this requirement, you must provide at least one direct link formatted as a specific identifier URL that points exactly to the official continent authority list at http://publications.europa.eu/resource/authority/continent.

label: spdx:Checksum
description: When defining a Checksum, you must specify exactly one algorithm used to produce the checksum, and this must be provided as a specific identifier URL. Additionally, you must provide exactly one checksum value, which represents the actual lower case hexadecimal encoded digest produced by that algorithm, and this must be recorded as a literal text value. Any violation of these requirements is considered a validation error and will be reported with a severity level of violation.

label: PlaceRestriction
description: When defining a Place restriction resource, the restriction must include at least one is in scheme relationship that provides a direct link to a specific identifier URL. This relationship must point exactly to the concept scheme located at http://publications.europa.eu/resource/authority/place, ensuring the place is properly categorized within that specific authoritative set of concepts.

label: dct:LicenseDocument
description: When cataloging a License Document, which serves as a legal document giving official permission to use a resource, you may specify at most one type to define the nature or genre of the license. If this type is provided, it must be expressed as a specific identifier URL that links to a standardized concept. Any violation of these requirements is considered a validation error and will be reported with a severity level of violation. Furthermore, to comply with strict license type restrictions, this concept must be explicitly categorized within a formal concept scheme. Specifically, it must contain at least one direct link indicating it belongs to the exact ADMS license type vocabulary located at http://purl.org/adms/licencetype/1.0.

label: MandatoryAgent
description: When managing records within the data catalog, you must define at least one mandatory agent. To satisfy this system requirement, the catalog data must contain at least one specific record that is explicitly classified as an agent.

label: MandatoryCatalog
description: When structuring your data catalog environment, it is strictly required to define at least one Catalog. Serving as a curated collection of metadata about resources like datasets and data services, the Catalog is a fundamental entity. To comply with this mandatory rule, your overall metadata submission must contain at least one explicit record that is formally classified as a Catalog.

label: MandatoryDataset
description: When curating your data catalog, it is mandatory to include at least one Dataset. A Dataset represents a collection of data that is published or curated by a single source and made available for access or download in one or more representations. To satisfy this strict structural requirement, the data graph must contain at least one specific instance explicitly declared as a Dataset.

label: CountryRestriction
description: When defining a Country restriction, which functions as a general resource, it must be explicitly categorized within a standardized vocabulary. To achieve this, the restriction must include at least one direct link indicating the concept scheme in which it is included. This relationship must point to a valid Concept Scheme, defined as a controlled set of concepts, and must specifically resolve to the exact identifier URL http://publications.europa.eu/resource/authority/country.

label: skos:Concept
description: When defining a Concept, which represents a specific idea, notion, or unit of thought within the catalog, you must provide at least one preferred label. To ensure proper formatting, this preferred label must be recorded as a literal text value rather than a web link or identifier. Any violation of these constraints is considered a validation error and will be reported with a severity level of violation.

label: dcat:Distribution
description: When defining a Distribution, which serves as a specific representation of a dataset, specific vocabulary constraints apply to its format and status properties. If a format is provided, it must be given as an identifier (URL) that refers to a predefined concept describing the file type, such as its format or medium. This concept must come from the official European Publications Office file type authority list at http://publications.europa.eu/resource/authority/file-type . Similarly, if a status is provided, it must also be given as an identifier (URL) referring to a predefined concept. This concept must be taken from the ADMS status vocabulary at http://purl.org/adms/status/1.0 . Any violation of these constraints is considered a validation error with severity level violation.

label: skos:ConceptScheme
description: When defining a Concept Scheme, which acts as a structured set of concepts and their semantic relationships, it is mandatory to provide at least one title. This title serves as the formal name given to the resource and must be recorded as a literal text value rather than a web address or internal reference. Any violation of these constraints is considered a validation error and will be reported with a severity level of violation.

label: dcat:Dataset
description: When curating a Dataset, which is a collection of data published or curated by a single source, several key properties may be defined using specific identifier URLs that link to standardized concepts.   The accrual periodicity, which describes the frequency with which items are added to the collection, when spesified, must be a direct link to a concept included in the official frequency authority concept scheme.   Likewise, If the language of the resource is provided, it must be specified via a direct link to a concept within the language authority concept scheme.   The publisher, representing the entity responsible for making the resource available, if provided, must also be a specific identifier URL pointing to a concept from the corporate body authority concept scheme.  If the spatial coverage of the resource is provided, you must provide a direct link to a concept that qualifies as either a country, a place, a continent, or a GeoNames restriction.   Finally, the theme, which acts as a main category for the resource, must be expressed as a direct link to a concept contained within the data theme authority concept scheme.

label: adms:Identifier
description: When configuring an Identifier record, which represents a complex identifier type based on standard data type catalogs, you may provide a maximum of one notation. This notation serves as the actual content string or classification code used to uniquely identify the concept within its specific scheme. If provided, this classification code must be recorded as a literal text value rather than a web link or specific identifier URL. Any violation of these constraints is considered a validation error and will be reported with a severity level of violation.

label: dcat:Catalog-1
description: When defining a Catalog, which serves as a curated collection of metadata about resources, several constraints apply.   The catalog must include at least one title and at least one description, both provided as text. It is also required to specify exactly one publisher responsible for making the resource available, which must be provided as a specific identifier URL. Furthermore, the catalog must list at least one dataset. If you choose to record the dates when the catalog was formally issued or last modified, you may provide at most one of each, and they must be formatted strictly as either a date or a date-time value.   The catalog can be structured hierarchically, meaning it may be included in at most one parent catalog, while any parts of a catalog must also be catalogs. It may also include catalog records that describe the registration of individual datasets or data services.   For legal and informational purposes, the catalog can link to at most one license document giving official permission for its use, at most one rights statement detailing intellectual property rights, and at most one homepage document. Finally, any specified languages, spatial coverage characteristics, or theme taxonomies used to classify the datasets must be provided as specific identifier URLs.  Any violation of the specified constraints results in a validation error with severity level Violation

label: dcat:CatalogRecord
description: For a Catalog Record, which describes the registration of a single dataset or data service within a data catalog, several strict curation rules must be followed. The record must specify exactly one primary topic, which must be categorized as a dataset. Additionally, it requires exactly one modification date indicating when the resource was changed, formatted strictly as either a date or a date-time value. The record may optionally include a single formal issuance date using the same date or date-time formatting. If the record is derived from a related resource, it may link to at most one source, which must itself be another catalog record. To indicate its workflow status, the record may provide at most one direct link to a defined concept. Furthermore, it may specify at most one established standard to which it conforms, represented as either an internal nested record or a direct link. Any titles given to the resource must be provided as literal text values, and any specified languages must be categorized as linguistic systems. Any violation of these constraints results in a validation error with severity level Violation.

label: foaf:Agent
description: When defining an Agent, which represents an entity such as a person, group, software, or physical artifact, you must provide at least one name as a literal text value. You may also specify a maximum of one type. If a type is provided, it must be formatted as a specific identifier URL that represents a concept. Furthermore, this type concept is subject to a publisher type restriction, meaning it must be explicitly included in a concept scheme via a specific identifier URL, and this scheme must specifically be the ADMS publisher type 1.0 scheme. Any violation of these constraints results in a validation error with severity level Violation.

label: dcat:Catalog
description: When defining a Catalog, which serves as a curated collection of metadata about resources, several properties must be carefully structured using specific identifier URLs. The language of the resource must be provided as a specific identifier URL pointing to a Concept that specifies at least one concept scheme exactly matching the Publications Office language authority (http://publications.europa.eu/resource/authority/language). Similarly, the publisher, which is the entity responsible for making the resource available, must be defined using a specific identifier URL linking to a Concept that includes at least one concept scheme explicitly set to the corporate body authority (http://publications.europa.eu/resource/authority/corporate-body). The spatial coverage, detailing the spatial characteristics of the resource, must also be a specific identifier URL pointing to a Concept that satisfies exactly one of the following geographic restrictions: a country restriction, a place restriction, a continent restriction, or a GeoNames restriction. Finally, the theme taxonomy, which is the knowledge organization system used to classify the catalog's datasets, must be provided as a specific identifier URL linking to a Concept Scheme that is strictly set to the exact value of the data theme authority (http://publications.europa.eu/resource/authority/data-theme).

label: dcat:Distribution-1
description: When defining a Distribution, which serves as a specific physical or digital representation of a dataset, you must provide at least one access URL as an identifier URL to ensure the resource can be reached. The distribution's title and description, if provided, must be expressed as literal text values.  To track its lifecycle, if provided, at most one date issued and at most one date modified are allowed, and they must be formatted strictly as either a date or a date-time value. If you provide a download URL for the file, it must be an identifier URL.  If provided, the file's format must be given as at most one identifier URL referring to a Media Type or Extent. Similarly, if provided, at most one media type is allowed and it must refer to a Media Type or Extent.  If provided, the distribution's byte size must be a decimal number with at most one value.  For legal and access governance, if provided, at most one license is allowed, which must be a License Document, and at most one rights statement is allowed.  If indicating an established standard the distribution conforms to, it must be provided as either an identifier URL or an internal nested record. Additionally, if provided, the language of the resource must refer to a Linguistic System, and any related web pages must refer to Documents.  To verify file integrity, if provided, at most one checksum is allowed.  Finally, if provided, the distribution's status of the distribution must be represented by at most one identifier URL referring to a Concept with at most one value.  Any violation of these constraints results in a validation error with severity level Violation.

label: dcat:Dataset-1
description: When curating a Dataset - defined as a collection of data published or curated by a single source - you must provide at least one title and at least one description as literal text values. If provided, at most one value is allowed for each of the following: the issued date, the modified date, and the version information. These dates must be formatted as either a date or a date-time value.If provided, the publisher must be an identifier URL referring to an Agent. Similarly, any accrual periodicity must be given as an identifier URL referring to a Frequency. If specified, access rights must refer to a Rights Statement, and any type must be represented by an identifier URL referring to a Concept.You can further describe the dataset by providing identifier URLs referring to languages, related resources, and Locations, as well as identifier URLs referring to Concepts that serve as its themes.The dataset may also be associated with Standards it conforms to, provided as identifier URLs or blank nodes, as well as Periods of Time and Provenance Statements.To facilitate access and user support, you may link to Distributions and sample Distributions, provide contact points, and reference Documents serving as landing pages or general informational pages.Finally, you can manage lineage and tracking by linking to other Datasets to indicate sources or versions, and by assigning Identifiers to the resource.Any violation of these constraints results in a validation error with severity level Violation.
